userStory: As a guest user, I want to access the Home page and use the available navigation actions, so that I can navigate to login, customer signup, clean survey, and site survey workflows.
applicationContext: The workflow starts by directly navigating to the application Home page at:

The Home page is expected to load in guest state without authentication. The workflow validates guest-state navigation options and transitions from the Home page to downstream pages through UI controls.
entryConditions: User is not authenticated and is operating in guest state.
Home page load completes successfully before interaction begins.
acceptanceCriteria: As a guest user, I want to access the Home page and use the available navigation actions, so that I can navigate to login, customer signup, clean survey, and site survey workflows.
The workflow starts by directly navigating to the application Home page at:

The Home page is expected to load in guest state without authentication. The workflow validates guest-state navigation options and transitions from the Home page to downstream pages through UI controls.
User is not authenticated and is operating in guest state.
Home page load completes successfully before interaction begins.
behaviorRules: The Home page must be accessible in guest state.
Home page navigation controls must be visible and enabled before interaction.
Selecting the Person/Profile button must navigate to the login page URL:
Selecting the Customer signup button must navigate to a URL containing:
Selecting the Clean survey button must navigate to a URL containing:
Selecting the Site survey button must navigate to a URL containing:
validationExpectations: Home page loads successfully at the specified entry URL.
Guest-state Home page renders without requiring authentication.
All listed Home page buttons are visible.
All listed Home page buttons are enabled and interactive.
Clicking each navigation control results in the expected page transition.
Resulting URLs contain the expected values or fragments.
transitionExpectations: Successful application launch transitions the user to the Home page in guest state.
Clicking the Person/Profile button transitions the user from Home to the Login page.
Clicking the Customer signup button transitions the user from Home to the customer-search page.
Clicking the Clean survey button transitions the user from Home to the vehicle-survey page.
Clicking the Site survey button transitions the user from Home to the survey page.
If navigation fails, the user remains on the Home page or does not reach the expected destination URL.
Verify the Home page loads in guest state.
Verify the following controls are visible and enabled:
reuseGuidance: Reuse the Home page resource and shared navigation components across manual and automation assets.
Reuse common button validation logic for visibility and enabled-state checks.
Reuse navigation verification resources for URL transition validation.
Downstream workflows for login, customer-search, vehicle-survey, and survey pages should own their internal page validations; this workflow should validate only successful navigation entry.
Use the provided Home page URL as the standardized workflow entry point.
Execute all validations in guest state.
Validate both UI state and resulting URL transitions.
Reuse approved URL fragments consistently across manual and automation assets.
Keep navigation verification isolated from downstream page business validations.
Treat each navigation path as an independent reusable workflow branch.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Home page loads successfully in guest state using the approved entry URL
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application loads the Home page successfully at http://localhost/washtabui/home in guest state without redirecting to a login page or requesting authentication.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Person/Profile button is visible and enabled on the guest Home page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Person/Profile button is visible, enabled, and available for interaction on the Home page in guest state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Back button is visible and enabled on the guest Home page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Back button is visible, enabled, and available for interaction on the Home page in guest state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Notification button is visible and enabled on the guest Home page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Notification button is visible, enabled, and available for interaction on the Home page in guest state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Home button is visible and enabled on the guest Home page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Home button is visible, enabled, and available for interaction on the Home page in guest state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Customer signup button is visible and enabled on the guest Home page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Customer signup button is visible, enabled, and available for interaction on the Home page in guest state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Clean survey button is visible and enabled on the guest Home page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Clean survey button is visible, enabled, and available for interaction on the Home page in guest state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Site survey button is visible and enabled on the guest Home page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Site survey button is visible, enabled, and available for interaction on the Home page in guest state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Person/Profile button navigates guest user to the login page URL
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application transitions from the Home page to the login page and the browser URL equals /washtabui/login?data=undefined.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify guest user can return to the Home page after navigating to the login page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The user returns to the Home page in guest state and the browser URL returns to http://localhost/washtabui/home.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Customer signup button navigates guest user to the customer-search workflow
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application transitions from the Home page to the customer-search workflow and the resulting URL contains customer-search.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Clean survey button navigates guest user to the vehicle-survey workflow
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application transitions from the Home page to the vehicle-survey workflow and the resulting URL contains vehicle-survey.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Site survey button navigates guest user to the survey workflow
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application transitions from the Home page to the survey workflow and the resulting URL contains survey?data.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Home button remains interactive when selected from the Home page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Home button remains interactive and the application stays on the Home page with the URL still showing http://localhost/washtabui/home.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify all primary guest navigation controls are simultaneously visible before interaction
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: All approved Home page navigation controls are displayed together on the Home page and available for guest user interaction.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify guest user remains on the Home page if login navigation does not complete successfully
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: If navigation does not reach /washtabui/login?data=undefined, the application remains on the Home page or does not complete the expected transition.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify guest user remains on the Home page if customer-search navigation does not complete successfully
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: If the resulting URL does not contain customer-search, the application does not complete the expected transition and the user remains on or returns to the Home page state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify guest user remains on the Home page if vehicle-survey navigation does not complete successfully
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: If the resulting URL does not contain vehicle-survey, the application does not complete the expected transition and the user remains on or returns to the Home page state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify guest user remains on the Home page if survey navigation does not complete successfully
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: If the resulting URL does not contain survey?data, the application does not complete the expected transition and the user remains on or returns to the Home page state.

validations: The application loads the Home page successfully at http://localhost/washtabui/home in guest state without redirecting to a login page or requesting authentication.
The Person/Profile button is visible, enabled, and available for interaction on the Home page in guest state.
The Back button is visible, enabled, and available for interaction on the Home page in guest state.
The Notification button is visible, enabled, and available for interaction on the Home page in guest state.
The Home button is visible, enabled, and available for interaction on the Home page in guest state.
The Customer signup button is visible, enabled, and available for interaction on the Home page in guest state.
The Clean survey button is visible, enabled, and available for interaction on the Home page in guest state.
The Site survey button is visible, enabled, and available for interaction on the Home page in guest state.
The application transitions from the Home page to the login page and the browser URL equals /washtabui/login?data=undefined.
The user returns to the Home page in guest state and the browser URL returns to http://localhost/washtabui/home.
The application transitions from the Home page to the customer-search workflow and the resulting URL contains customer-search.
The application transitions from the Home page to the vehicle-survey workflow and the resulting URL contains vehicle-survey.
The application transitions from the Home page to the survey workflow and the resulting URL contains survey?data.
The Home button remains interactive and the application stays on the Home page with the URL still showing http://localhost/washtabui/home.
All approved Home page navigation controls are displayed together on the Home page and available for guest user interaction.
If navigation does not reach /washtabui/login?data=undefined, the application remains on the Home page or does not complete the expected transition.
If the resulting URL does not contain customer-search, the application does not complete the expected transition and the user remains on or returns to the Home page state.
If the resulting URL does not contain vehicle-survey, the application does not complete the expected transition and the user remains on or returns to the Home page state.
If the resulting URL does not contain survey?data, the application does not complete the expected transition and the user remains on or returns to the Home page state.

wrapperExpectations: Reuse approved shared/common wrapper keywords before low-level SeleniumLibrary actions.
Do not invent suite-level custom keywords; compose from approved upstream resource knowledge.

mustNotInvent: duplicate ownership for controls already owned in approved upstream resources
hardcoded workflow routing or mapped page logic in Python
downstreamGuidance.generationRule: Downstream generation must consume approved workflow knowledge plus approved resource context before creating new artifacts.

unresolvedGaps: Element name is derived from the raw DOM id and is not business-meaningful for downstream automation assets.
Element name is overly technical and exposes implementation detail instead of business intent.
Element name uses raw DOM terminology instead of user-facing semantics.
Element name is implementation-centric and weak for reusable keyword generation.
Element name is inconsistent, lower-quality, and difficult to reuse semantically.
Element name is tied to DOM naming conventions rather than workflow intent.
Element name is technical and inconsistent with business-readable automation naming standards.
Locator xpath=//a is extremely generic and likely to match multiple links, making the element brittle and unsafe for automation reuse.
This anchor element appears to represent the same Home navigation control already modeled by auto_home_btn_button.
Background container is decorative/layout-only and provides little reusable automation value.
Underlying native control is rendered as an anchor element rather than a standard button.

The page model does not include any explicit guest-state indicator, page heading, or page-state validation element to support stronger observable Home page verification beyond URL and control visibility.
